summary: Ukrainian forces struck three key bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, while Russian forces reported the liberation of Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk. Putin announced a proposal to limit combat to four regions, and Russia moved its Donetsk capture deadline to 31 December. Multiple UAV and drone attacks continued across the frontlines, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.
key_points: Ukrainian forces damaged an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk in Donetsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
President Putin said Russia would consider limiting hostilities to Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, DNR and LNR, citing Ukrainian personnel shortages (Source: @UkraineNow/82260).
Russia shifted its deadline for capturing Donetsk region to 31 December, according to President Zelensky's statement (Source: @UkraineNow/82276).
Guided aviation bombs were launched by tactical aviation over Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66824).
A Russian FPV drone attack on a minibus in Zaporizhzhia killed two and injured six, including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82257).
Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31242).
Russian troops announced the liberation of Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243).
Two Russian agents were detained by the SBU for planning a car bomb attack in central Kyiv (Source: @UkraineNow/82258).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces struck three key bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, disrupting Russian supply lines. Putin announced a proposal to limit combat to four regions, while Russia moved its Donetsk capture deadline to 31 December. UAV and drone activity continued across multiple fronts, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.

points: Ukrainian forces hit an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk in Donetsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, targeting Russian logistics routes (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
The SBU detained two Russian agents suspected of planning a car bomb attack on a Ukrainian military vehicle near Maidan Nezalezhnosti in Kyiv (Source: @UkraineNow/82258).
A Russian FPV drone attack on a minibus in Zaporizhzhia resulted in two deaths and six injuries, including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82257).
Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka, causing unknown casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31242).
A Russian drone struck the Zaporizhzhia regional administration building, damaging the facility (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31252).
Heatwaves increased power demand, prompting scheduled electricity curtailments across all Ukrainian regions (Source: @UkraineNow/82272).
President Putin proposed limiting hostilities to Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, DNR and LNR, claiming Ukrainian personnel shortages (Source: @UkraineNow/82260).
Russia extended its deadline for the capture of Donetsk region to 31 December (Source: @UkraineNow/82276).
Russian troops announced the liberation of Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243).
Guided aviation bombs were deployed by tactical aviation over Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66824).
Ukrainian forces attacked a private enterprise in Dnipro, resulting in seven injuries, including a seriously wounded 22‑year‑old (Source: @UkraineNow/82255).
Russian forces reported increased activity of tactical aviation on the north‑east and south‑east fronts (Source: @kpszsu/66823).
Ukrainian strikes damaged bridges near Novoazovsk (Donetsk) and two railway bridges in Luhansk, hindering Russian troop movements (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
A Russian drone attack in Kharkiv produced a visible smoke column, indicating possible target engagement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31250).
UAVs were reported heading toward Snovsk in Chernihiv from the north (Source: @kpszsu/66808).
Reactive UAVs launched from Sumy moved westward toward Poltava (Source: @kpszsu/66820).
UAV activity from the Black Sea targeted Mykolaiv, heading northward (Source: @kpszsu/66821).
UAVs were observed moving toward the Black Sea direction of Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/66819).
UAVs reported heading toward Snovsk in Chernihiv from the north (Source: @kpszsu/66808).
Reactive UAVs from Sumy moved westward toward Poltava (Source: @kpszsu/66820).

UAV from the Black Sea attacked Mykolaiv (source: @kpszsu/66815).

UAV from the Black Sea headed toward Chornomorsk (source: @kpszsu/66819).

UAVs reported heading toward the southern direction of the Sea towards the city of Pivdenne (source: @eRadarrua/98633).
Guided aviation bombs were launched by tactical aviation over Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66824).
Tactical aviation activity increased on the north‑east and south‑east fronts, raising the threat of air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/66823).
Russian FPV drone attacks on civilian vehicles in Zaporizhzhia caused casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/82257).
A Russian drone struck the Zaporizhzhia regional administration building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31252).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces damaged an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
President Putin announced a proposal to limit combat to Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, DNR and LNR, citing Ukrainian personnel shortages (Source: @UkraineNow/82260).
Russia extended its deadline for capturing Donetsk region to 31 December (Source: @UkraineNow/82276).
Guided aviation bombs were deployed by tactical aviation over Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66824).
A Russian FPV drone attack on a minibus in Zaporizhzhia killed two and injured six, including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82257).
Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31242).
Two Russian agents were detained by the SBU for planning a car bomb attack in central Kyiv (Source: @UkraineNow/82258).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian strikes on bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk indicate a focus on disrupting Russian supply lines, while Russia's proposal to limit combat zones suggests a strategic shift to consolidate gains. Continued UAV and drone activity from maritime and land bases across multiple fronts raises the risk of both military and civilian casualties. The detention of Russian agents planning attacks in Kyiv highlights ongoing covert operations aimed at destabilizing Ukrainian urban centers.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing bridge defenses and increasing air defense alerts in response to heightened UAV activity. Russian statements about extending deadlines and limiting combat areas may be aimed at managing domestic expectations and reallocating forces. International observers note the escalation of drone warfare as a key factor influencing front‑line dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch points, especially from the Black Sea, should be prioritized to anticipate future strikes on coastal and inland targets. The bridge attacks suggest Ukrainian intelligence has identified critical Russian logistics nodes. The SBU's successful interception of Russian agents underscores effective counter‑intelligence operations within Kyiv.
